reasoning: PKGBUILD uses trusted source with pinned checksum, no dangerous commands in build/install scope. Skipped .deb file is expected for binary packages and poses no execution risk during makepkg.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped audit of pgadmin4-desktop-9.16-x86_64.deb binary
No review of potential maintainer scripts inside .deb (though not executed during makepkg)
false_negatives: Models didn't flag reliance on external binary without source audit - inherent limitation of -bin packages
learnings: Improve binary package handling: Explicitly note in reports that -bin packages inherit upstream security risks
Prioritize review of any .install hooks or custom scripts over binary files
Add warning when package contains unaudited binaries from third-party sources
Enhance PKGBUILD analysis to detect checksum verification gaps
